title: First Day Back on Campus
description: Stepped into a new semester with fresh goals, a packed planner, and the energy that only the first week can bring.
fullDescription: The first Monday of the semester always feels cinematic. The halls were full again, everyone was swapping internship stories, and I remember finding a sunny corner near the library to plan everything I wanted to build that term. It set the tone for the rest of the year: ambitious, slightly chaotic, and genuinely exciting.

title: Late Library Study Group
description: A simple revision session turned into one of those nights where everyone stayed far longer than planned.
fullDescription: We started with one whiteboard and two chapters to review, then somehow the group grew as more classmates joined after dinner. By midnight the room was full of sticky notes, half-finished equations, and bad jokes. It was one of those memories that reminded me college is as much about the people you solve problems with as the grades themselves.

title: Hackathon Win
description: Built an AI assistant in 24 hours and somehow still had enough energy left to pitch it on stage.
fullDescription: Our team spent the night building an AI workflow that summarized lengthy PDFs into action-oriented notes. We were running on caffeine, stubbornness, and exactly zero extra time when the final submission window closed. Hearing our project announced as the winner felt surreal, mostly because we had already started planning what we would fix after the event ended.

title: Campus Cultural Night
description: An evening of performances, food stalls, and the kind of campus energy that makes everything feel bigger.
fullDescription: Cultural night was loud, colorful, and impossible to walk through quickly because every corner had something happening. I spent most of the evening moving between performances and trying different stalls with friends from class. The whole night felt like a reminder that college memories are built just as much in celebration as they are in classrooms.

title: Road Trip After Finals
description: The classic post-exam escape: music too loud, snacks everywhere, and zero plans beyond the next turn.
fullDescription: As soon as finals ended, we packed the car and drove out of the city without a detailed itinerary. The day blurred into mountain overlooks, convenience store coffee, and playlists that everyone insisted on controlling. It was one of those trips that felt unimportant while it was happening and unforgettable the moment it ended.

title: Capstone Project Presentation
description: Presented months of design work, prototypes, and user feedback in one of the most nerve-racking classes of the year.
fullDescription: The presentation was the payoff for weeks of iteration. We refined slides until the last minute, rehearsed transitions in empty classrooms, and kept rewriting the presentation script so it sounded effortless. Walking out after the Q and A felt like exhaling after holding my breath for an entire semester.

title: First Internship Showcase Day
description: My first startup internship ended with a showcase day where I presented work that had moved beyond class assignments into real product space.
fullDescription: Internship showcase day made everything feel suddenly real. Instead of course rubrics and professor feedback, I was presenting a feature to a room full of engineers and product managers who had actually used it. It was intimidating, but it also made the path after graduation feel a lot less abstract.

title: Late-Night Coding Sprint
description: One bug turned into an all-night sprint, but the final build finally clicked just before sunrise.
fullDescription: The plan was to fix one broken animation and go to sleep early. Instead, the team stayed up rewriting half the interaction flow and ordering midnight food. Somewhere around 4 a.m. the app finally felt right, and the weird mixture of exhaustion and satisfaction made the whole night memorable.

title: Graduation Prep Photo Walk
description: A slow afternoon around campus, taking photos and realizing how many familiar places were about to become memories.
fullDescription: What started as a casual photo walk turned into a sentimental loop through the spaces that had quietly shaped the last few years. We stopped at the library stairs, the old lab building, and the coffee place where every group project eventually ended up. It felt like documenting a map of our college life before we all stepped into whatever came next.

title: Rooftop Farewell Hangout
description: A low-key evening with the people who were there for the deadlines, the wins, and the ordinary days in between.
fullDescription: Near the end of the year, we met on a rooftop with takeout, a speaker, and no real schedule. Most of the conversation was just retelling shared stories: the deadline disasters, the internships, the trips, and the tiny routines that had become part of everyday life. It was simple, and that is exactly why it mattered.
